seed: safety vs exposure; pressure_vs_release; split_vertical; echo_right; braid against ripple; pressure_border; thin_vs_heavy; vessel; softness_severity | abstract variation request 315; target batch count 500
title: Echoes of Safety and Exposure
reasoning: This abstract composition embodies the tension between safety and exposure through a palette of shelter green, warm brown, cold white, and open blue. The contrast lies in the soft, warm earthy tones occupying the left diagonal wedge, representing safety and stability, while the cooler tones of pale skin and cold white surge on the right, echoing exposure and vulnerability. The central area where these two emotions collide is intentionally disrupted with broken pixels, suggesting imperfection and the fragility of safety. The 'stack' pattern on the safety side juxtaposes with the 'tide' pattern on the exposure side, visually representing their interplay and conflict. This arrangement evokes a narrative of being simultaneously shielded and exposed, with the rich color contrast amplifying the emotional tension.

layout.name: diagonal_argument
layout.description: Emotion A occupies one diagonal wedge while emotion B presses against the opposite wedge.

composition.name: exposure_vs_shelter
composition.idea: A pale exposed center is either protected by a frame or left open to incoming marks.

referenceExample.title: Wonder Through Rain
referenceExample.pattern: muted blue rain field interrupted by a small floating glow

generationRules: Pick 1 pattern type for emotion A.
Pick 1 pattern type for emotion B.
Pick 1 shape seed as the central object.
Apply 1 to 6 broken pixels to create imperfection.
Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the mood is panic, rage, urgency, euphoria, or pressure.
Use the shape seed as an abstract organizing metaphor, not as a literal icon.
Make the two selected pattern types visibly collide, overlap, interrupt, or trade space.
Prefer 4 to 8 distinct hex colors, with at least one quiet field color and one accent color.
Keep the 8x8 grid readable at thumbnail size by preserving clear large-scale structure.
Let one emotion dominate 55 to 70 percent of the square unless the selected layout demands balance.
Use broken pixels as intentional disruptions, scars, repairs, leaks, or false echoes.
Avoid perfectly even distribution unless the chosen composition is control, serenity, clarity, or certainty.
Give the main collision a location: center, edge, corner, diagonal, border, or channel.
generationSpec.negativeSpaceRule: Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the chosen emotional tone needs dense pressure.
